text.title: aws-lambda Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for aws-lambda as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Initializing boto3 clients, database connections, or external API clients inside the handler function.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Connection setup runs on every invocation, multiplying latency and cost. With INIT phase billed since August 2025, functions with heavy initialization inside the handler pay double.

failure-mode.description: SQS event source mapping without a DLQ configured on the queue.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Messages that exhaust all retries are silently dropped if there is no DLQ. Failed processing leaves no trace for debugging or replay.

failure-mode.description: Lambda functions that exceed 15-minute timeout for long-running batch jobs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lambda has a hard 15-minute timeout. Jobs that approach this limit should be migrated to ECS Fargate tasks or Step Functions activities which support arbitrary runtimes.
